english_track_name: Climate change; Geo-Engineering; Industrial Environmental Sustainability
degree_class: LM-35 (Environmental Engineering / Ingegneria per l'ambiente e il territorio)
admission_test_type: Master-level evaluation of admission requirements by the Academic Committee; no programme-specific written entrance test is published.
applicant_route: Non-EU qualification with legal residence in Italy
Non-EU qualification while residing abroad and requiring a study visa (the route relevant for Turkish applicants living abroad)
admission_call_round: 1st call for applicants with a non-Italian qualification: 19 December 2025 to 13 February 2026, 2:00 p.m. Italian time; results 15 April 2026
2nd call for applicants with a non-Italian qualification: 18 March 2026 to 20 April 2026, 2:00 p.m. Italian time; results 22 June 2026
A separate international-tracks call (24 April 2026 to 18 May 2026) is published for Physics of Complex Systems and Nanotechnologies for ICTs MNIS, not for this programme
application_platform: Apply@polito for the admission application; UNIVERSITALY for study-visa pre-enrolment by non-EU applicants residing abroad

academic_requirements.qualification: Applicants must hold a Bachelor's degree (EQF level 6) or an equivalent academic qualification awarded by an accredited university and giving access to second-cycle studies.
academic_requirements.schooling_and_credits: Applicants still enrolled in a foreign long-cycle degree may apply if they meet the minimum requirement of 15 years of global schooling, at least 3 years at university level, and at least 180 ECTS credits or equivalent.
academic_requirements.curricular_assessment: Applicants must meet the specific curricular requirements and have academic performance considered suitable for admission; the Academic Committee verifies whether the previous university studies are adequate and consistent with the chosen programme.
academic_requirements.track_specific_background: The public A.Y. 2026/2027 course-specific requirements PDF explicitly lists 'ENVIRONMENTAL AND LAND ENGINEERING Climate Change' with recommended background 'Any field of Engineering'. Separate 2026/2027 background rows for Geo-Engineering and Industrial Environmental Sustainability were not found in the checked admissions PDF. [uncertain]

country_specific_eligibility: Turkish applicants with a Turkish Bachelor's degree are treated as applicants with a non-Italian qualification; if they reside abroad they must also complete UNIVERSITALY pre-enrolment for a type D study visa. For enrolment, PoliTo accepts a Declaration of Value, a CIMEA Statement of Comparability, or a Diploma Supplement, and may request additional verification documents such as a CIMEA Statement of Verification.
minimum_background_or_credits: At least 180 ECTS credits or equivalent and a qualification giving access to second-cycle studies are required. The only programme-specific background published in the checked 2026/2027 sources is 'Any field of Engineering' for the Climate change English track. [uncertain]

language_requirements.english_tracks: For English-taught tracks, PoliTo's general rule is English level B2 or higher. The A.Y. 2026/2027 course-specific requirements PDF lists IELTS 5.5 for 'Environmental and Land Engineering Climate Change'. The curriculum and specialist-track pages show complete English study plans for Climate change, Geo-Engineering, and Industrial Environmental Sustainability. [uncertain]
language_requirements.italian_track_rule: For Italian-taught or partially Italian/English programmes, PoliTo requires English level B2 or higher plus Italian level B2 or higher. This matters for the Italian-only track within this bilingual programme.
language_requirements.accepted_certificates: Accepted certificates for English and Italian are listed in PoliTo's official CLA certificate charts.
language_requirements.exemptions: English-certificate exemptions include IB Diploma taught entirely in English, eligible European School diplomas with L1 English, qualifications awarded by institutions from specified English-medium countries, and approved medium-of-instruction cases listed on the official admissions page.

english_certificate_codes: For TOEFL/TOEIC certificates, use institution code C285. For Cambridge certificates, share the result with Politecnico di Torino and provide the Candidate ID number.
language_submission_timing: The English and/or Italian certificate is part of the Apply@polito application documents. If the certificate status appears 'PENDING' after upload, PoliTo states that it will be verified during the evaluation process.

application_deadline_eu.note: For applicants with a non-Italian qualification who do not need a visa (EU applicants and non-EU applicants already residing in Italy), the same Apply@polito admission calls apply.
application_deadline_eu.first_call: 19 December 2025 to 13 February 2026, 2:00 p.m. Italian time; results 15 April 2026.
application_deadline_eu.second_call: 18 March 2026 to 20 April 2026, 2:00 p.m. Italian time; results 22 June 2026.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment: 1st-semester online enrolment closes 5 October 2026. 2nd-semester online enrolment for EU and non-EU applicants residing in Italy runs from 13 November 2026 to 24 March 2027.

application_deadline_non_eu.first_call: Applications open 19 December 2025 and close 13 February 2026 at 2:00 p.m. Italian time; results 15 April 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.second_call: Applications open 18 March 2026 and close 20 April 2026 at 2:00 p.m. Italian time; results 22 June 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.enrolment: 1st-semester online enrolment closes 5 October 2026; mandatory enrolment documents are due by 5 October 2026; the study visa type D upload deadline is 30 November 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.note: This is the relevant route for Turkish applicants residing abroad and requiring a visa.

universitaly_preenrolment_deadline: The dedicated 2026/2027 visa section says non-EU applicants residing abroad must submit UNIVERSITALY pre-enrolment by 30 May 2026 for the 1st call and 15 July 2026 for the 2nd call. An earlier deadlines table on the same page shows 15 May 2026 as the 1st-call study-visa pre-enrolment deadline. [uncertain]
application_fee: EUR 50.00 non-refundable application fee paid online by credit card via PagoPA.
required_documents: Passport (other IDs are accepted only for EU nationals)
Residence permit (only for non-EU citizens living in Italy)
Bachelor's degree certificate or an official university document stating the provisional graduation date
Syllabus / course descriptions; the programme-specific 2026/2027 PDF appears to mark this as Optional for the Climate change row. [uncertain]
English and/or Italian language certificate, depending on the chosen track and language route
Declaration of Value of the Bachelor's degree or a CIMEA Statement of Comparability (compulsory for enrolment if available at application stage)
Optional additional document such as a GRE General Test score report, recommendation letter, motivation letter, training certificate, or relevant work-experience proof
document_translation_authentication: Documents must be issued on letterheaded paper, stamped and signed by the issuing institution. Documents in Italian, English, French, or Spanish are accepted; other languages require the original plus an official English or Italian translation in one file. For enrolment, translations into Italian must be certified by the Italian Embassy in the issuing country or sworn before an Italian court translator. PoliTo refers applicants to the CIMEA glossary for legalization guidance.

entry_exam_or_test.type: No written entrance exam is published for this programme.
entry_exam_or_test.selection_method: Admission is by evaluation of qualifications, curricular requirements, academic performance, and language documents by the Academic Committee.
entry_exam_or_test.programme_specific_note: The checked 2026/2027 course-specific requirements row for 'Environmental and Land Engineering Climate Change' does not publish an interview or GRE requirement. [uncertain]

test_score_or_threshold: No numeric admission-test threshold is published. The explicit programme-linked threshold found in the checked 2026/2027 sources is the English requirement of IELTS 5.5 for 'Environmental and Land Engineering Climate Change'. [uncertain]

source_quotes.quote: The Master’s degree programme is taught in Italian and in English.

The entire track will be provided in English.

July 15, 2026, if you submitted the application in the second call
Please upload the documents listed below: Passport ... Curriculum Vitae ... Official Transcript of Records

uncertainty_notes: Programme-level English status is not ambiguous: the official programme-details page says the master's degree programme is taught in Italian and in English, so is_fully_english_programme is false.

The curriculum and specialist-track pages confirm complete English study plans for three named tracks: Climate change, Geo-Engineering, and Industrial Environmental Sustainability. A fourth track, 'Gestione dei rischi naturali', appears in Italian on the curriculum page, so the programme remains bilingual overall while still offering complete English tracks.

The A.Y. 2026/2027 course-specific requirements PDF explicitly names only 'ENVIRONMENTAL AND LAND ENGINEERING Climate Change' with English / IELTS 5.5 / Any field of Engineering. Geo-Engineering and Industrial Environmental Sustainability were confirmed as full-English tracks from the curriculum and specialist-track pages rather than from a separate admissions-PDF row.

The 2026/2027 admissions page has a conflict for the 1st-call study-visa pre-enrolment date: the deadlines table shows 15 May 2026, while the dedicated UNIVERSITALY visa section states 30 May 2026. The dedicated visa section was used in universitaly_preenrolment_deadline and the conflict is preserved as uncertainty.

No programme-specific written entrance test, interview requirement, or GRE requirement was published in the checked sources for Environmental and Land Engineering. The programme is therefore recorded as admission by academic evaluation only.

The checked programme-specific PDF row appears to mark syllabus/course descriptions as 'Optional' for the Climate change row; the public sources checked did not publish a separate mandatory-document row for the other English tracks.
